books.title: The Teeth of the Tiger
books.author: Maurice Leblanc
books.summary: A series of murders connected to an enormous legacy leads to a battle against an unseen adversary.

books.title: The Purple Land
books.author: W. H. Hudson
books.summary: An Englishman becomes embroiled in adventure, politics, and romance in 19th-century Uruguay.

books.title: The Diary
books.author: Samuel Pepys
books.summary: The diary of a man living in London in the 17th century.

books.title: Queen Victoria
books.author: Lytton Strachey
books.summary: A sardonic and generally unflattering biography of Victoria Alexandrina, Queen of England.

books.title: Harding’s Luck
books.author: E. Nesbit
books.summary: A poor orphan boy accidentally discovers magic which sends him into the past and leads to his recovery of a fortune and a lordly title.

books.title: The School for Scandal
books.author: Richard Brinsley Sheridan
books.summary: Despite their best efforts, the rumormongers of 18th-century London are disappointed by their targets.

books.title: Philoctetes
books.author: Sophocles
books.summary: A wounded warrior, abandoned on a deserted island, is visited by former comrades who ask for his help in winning a war.

books.title: Russian Folktales
books.author: A. N. Afanasyev
books.summary: A collection of Russian folktales compiled by A. N. Afanasyev, translated and edited by Leonard A. Magnus.

books.title: Our Mutual Friend
books.author: Charles Dickens
books.summary: When the body of an unknown man is dragged from the river Thames, a series of complications involving the will of a wealthy man entangle the lives of several Londoners.

books.title: John Silence Stories
books.author: Algernon Blackwood
books.summary: A physician turned detective uses his psychic abilities to solve the strange cases presented to him.

books.title: A Personal Record
books.author: Joseph Conrad
books.summary: Conrad recounts his childhood in Poland, his sailing experiences, and the work on his debut novel.

books.title: The Eye of Osiris
books.author: R. Austin Freeman
books.summary: A newly qualified doctor helps his former teacher, a famed forensic scientist, unravel a case involving ancient Egyptian artifacts, a missing person, and a mysterious will.

books.title: In Darkest London
books.author: Ada Elizabeth Chesterton
books.summary: A social campaigner describes her experiences living on the streets of 1920s London.

books.title: The Social Contract
books.author: Jean-Jacques Rousseau
books.summary: Rousseau explores the political philosophy of authority originating from the consent of the people.

books.title: Gitanjali
books.author: Rabindranath Tagore
books.summary: Prose poems of offering translated by the author from the original Bengali.

books.title: The Devil’s Dictionary
books.author: Ambrose Bierce
books.summary: A witty, sharp collection of sardonic word definitions from the 1800s and early 1900s that provide a snapshot into the irreverent humor of early American culture.

books.title: The Time Machine
books.author: H. G. Wells
books.summary: A mysterious inventor travels to a land far off in time.

books.title: Golf Stories
books.author: P. G. Wodehouse
books.summary: A collection of Wodehouse’s golf stories featuring the Oldest Member.

books.title: Barlaam and Ioasaph
books.author: Anonymous
books.summary: A monk converts a cloistered young prince to Christianity.

books.title: To Cuba and Back
books.author: Richard Henry Dana Jr.
books.summary: A captivating travel memoir recounting an 1859 journey to Cuba, offering vivid descriptions of the island’s culture, landscapes, and people.
